body.body:
Sub-slice of T-experiment-primitive (#456). Build the scheduler/runner that takes Experiment objects from the schema (shipped 2026-04-14 as T-experiment-primitive-schema, commit 64e1d472) and actually executes them.

## Scope (MVP)

- `ExperimentScheduler` class in `wild_igor/igor/cognition/experiment_scheduler.py`
- Enqueue: accept an Experiment in status=PROPOSED, persist to a queue
- Tick: pick next ready experiment, advance to RUNNING, dispatch probe, record Observation, mark OBSERVED
- Probe dispatch by ProbeKind:
  - memory_query → cortex search (cheap, always allowed)
  - db_query → db_proxy execute_read (cheap, always allowed)
  - tool_call → registry lookup + invocation (medium cost; whitelist enforced)
  - habit_dryrun → not yet implemented (defer, ABORT with reason)
  - channel_send → defer in MVP (ABORT with reason)
  - sim_turn → defer (ABORT with reason)
- Cost/timeout: per-experiment soft timeout (default 30s); on timeout → abort
- TWM push of OBSERVED experiment outcome at salience 0.6 with cp1_provisional=True
- Persistence: Postgres `experiment_queue` table — id, experiment_json, status, enqueued_at, started_at, completed_at

## Out of scope (later sub-slices)

- Outcome → engram update (that's T-experiment-primitive-outcome-feedback)
- Mode-aware scheduling (AWAKE foreground vs BACKGROUND batch vs SLEEP consolidation) — pick simplest first
- Risk tier gating to Akien approval — flag in the code, don't implement until Akien decides
- Cockpit integration (T-experiment-primitive-cockpit-integration)
- CP-axiom audit check (T-experiment-primitive-cp-check)

## Open design choices made under CP2 (revise later if needed)

- Storage: dedicated `experiment_queue` table, NOT main memories table. Reason: experiments are operational state during their lifecycle; the OBSERVED outcome can be deposited as a memory after the fact (in the outcome-feedback sub-slice). Keeps the memories table from churning with mid-flight experiments.
- Lifecycle: experiments CAN be aborted mid-probe (CP2 license; biology says abandon freely)
- Outcome judgment: Igor decides via Observation(outcome=...) on his own. External oracle path is later.
- Risk tiers: MVP whitelist of safe probe kinds; flag higher tiers for later.

## Dependencies

- T-experiment-primitive-schema ✅ (Experiment dataclass quartet shipped 2026-04-14)

## Tests

- enqueue + tick happy path (memory_query probe)
- tick on empty queue is no-op
- failed probe → Observation(outcome=MISMATCH) with error captured
- timeout → ABORTED with reason
- whitelist enforcement: unsupported probe kind → ABORT (not crash)
- TWM push fires on OBSERVED with cp1_provisional flag
- Persistence roundtrip (enqueue, restart scheduler, ensure experiment is recovered)
body.title: Experiment primitive: queue + runner (sub-slice of #456)
body.result: ExperimentScheduler with Postgres queue + probe dispatch (memory/db/tool) + CP6 whitelist + INCONCLUSIVE-on-error + timeout abort + TWM push w/ cp1_provisional. 17 tests, 2284 total pass.
